notes: COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S) By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete: 1) The L&S Essential Skills Requirements: Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language; OR 2) IGETC IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond Completion of Essential Skills requirements (R&C, QR, and FL) or IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of the Essential Skills requirements is strongly recommended prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley. For more information on minimum UC eligibility requirements for transfer applicants for fall 2025 and beyond, please visit the UC Berkeley transfer requirements page . Major Requirements: See details on preparation for this major below. Primary selection criteria for admission, in general: - completion of L&S Requirements (or IGETC), plus - strength of academic preparation, and - grade point average. For more information on admission to UC Berkeley: http://admissions.berkeley.edu For more information on majors at UC Berkeley: Berkeley Academic Guide: http://guide.berkeley.edu

Fall 2023 Admission For this Major: Transfer students who select this major on their College of Letters and Science (L&S) application and are offered admission to L&S will be directly admitted into this major. If you are interested in this major, it is very important to select this major on your application, and complete as many lower-division major requirements as possible prior to transfer. See details on preparation for this major below. LOWER DIVISION REQUIREMENTS FOR GEOGRAPHY MAJORS The major requires a student take three lower division courses, one from each of these areas: PHYSICAL GEOGRAPHY GEOG 40 (Introduction to Earth System Science) or ESPM 15 (Introduction to Environmental Sciences) or Complete a course approved for the Physical Geography requirement listed below. This covers physical geography basics. WORLD GEOGRAPHY GEOG 10AC (Worldlings: Regions, Peoples, and States) or GEOG 20 (Globalization) or GEOG 31 (Justice, Nature, and the Geographies of Identity) or Complete a course approved for the World Geography requirement listed below. This covers world geography. REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY GEOG C32 (Introduction to Global Studies) or GEOG 50AC (California) or GEOG C55 (Introduction to Central Asia) or GEOG 70AC (The Urban Experience) or GEOG 72AC (The Bay Area) or Complete a course approved for the Regional Geography requirement listed below. This represents regional geographies. AP Exams do not satisfy any major requirements for Geography; IBHL Geography exam with a passing score of 5, 6, or 7 can be used to satisfy GEOG 20. Courses other than those listed below may be acceptable. Transfer students who have taken introductory courses elsewhere should consult with the departmental undergraduate advisor at Berkeley to avoid repeating lower division work. For more information about the major: https://geography.berkeley.edu/
